title: Add narrow stale systemd cleanup privilege
goal: Provide a narrow fail-closed privileged path, or explicit manual runbook, for clearing stale failed systemd unit state such as user@111.service without broad sudo.

acceptance.assertion: The privileged path cannot restart, stop, enable, disable or edit arbitrary services.
It can only clear failed state for an explicitly allowed target or class after read-only status checks.
The Heim-PC audit can clear stale user@111.service without granting general sudo, or the runbook records the exact manual command.

metadata.live_audit_finding: system systemd remained degraded because user@111.service stale fail could not be reset without sudo; broker had no reset-failed action.
metadata.block_reason: Four terminal system units are bounded reset-failed candidates after read-only classification, but the Grabowski runtime is NoNewPrivileges-hardened, direct sudo cannot elevate, and the recovery-gated Rootbroker rejects eligible calls as stale under T030. Bypassing that gate is not authorized.
metadata.next_action: After T030 is deployed, re-read exact unit properties and execute only systemctl reset-failed for the unchanged allowlist; then prove systemctl --failed empty.

lease_scope_migration.rationale: The privileged cleanup path belongs to Grabowski/heim-pc; Bureau participation is only this exact task record.
does_not_establish: safe mutation of terminal historical tasks
